explanation: Substituted '/' with '-' for command-line flags, which is an equivalent syntax for some Windows utilities.
Inserted quotes around the full path to handle spaces or special characters, which is a common Windows command-line parsing behavior.
Omitted the full path to the executable, assuming it is in the system PATH, which is a common and valid simplification.
Reordered the arguments '/sc 1' and '/gui 0', which are not dependent on order for the psr.exe utility.
Wrapped the command in cmd /c to simulate execution in a controlled environment, which is a safe test-only technique.
